pairs.question: When and where was the 15th Rome Grand Prix held?
pairs.answer: The 15th Rome Grand Prix was held on 19 May 1963 at the ACI Vallelunga Circuit near Rome, Italy.

pairs.question: How many laps did each heat of the 15th Rome Grand Prix consist of?
pairs.answer: Each heat of the 15th Rome Grand Prix consisted of 40 laps of the circuit.

pairs.question: Which driver won both heats of the 15th Rome Grand Prix?
pairs.answer: British driver Bob Anderson won both heats of the 15th Rome Grand Prix in a Lola Mk4.

pairs.question: What was the outcome of the first heat for Franco Bernabei in the 15th Rome Grand Prix?
pairs.answer: Franco Bernabei was leading the first heat in his De Tomaso when his engine blew up, preventing him from participating in the second heat.

pairs.question: Why was Jo Siffert unable to participate in the 15th Rome Grand Prix?
pairs.answer: Jo Siffert was denied a visa to race in Rome by the Swiss Automobile Club, despite having planned to compete in the event.

pairs.question: Can you provide a code snippet to generate a list of the top three finishers for each heat in the 15th Rome Grand Prix?
pairs.answer:

pairs.question: What type of racing rules was the 15th Rome Grand Prix run under?
pairs.answer: The 15th Rome Grand Prix was run to Formula One rules.
